topic: Claude Mythos for Cybersecurity: Zero-Day Detection and Project Glasswing Guide

key_points: Anthropic's official Claude Mythos Preview page with full technical specifications
Details on controlled evaluation methodology and cyber capability demonstrations
Explains the decision to restrict access through Project Glasswing
Covers the 32-step corporate network attack simulation results
Practical playbook for security teams on operationalizing Mythos-level AI scanning
Covers workflow integration for enterprise security teams
Addresses how to scale AI vulnerability discovery across large codebases
Discusses risk management and governance for AI-powered security tools
Analysis of how Mythos redefines enterprise cybersecurity strategy
Industry expert perspectives on the offensive/defensive dual-use dilemma
Coverage of the invite-only consortium model and partner screening process
Discussion of implications for CISOs and security leadership
Independent UK government AI Safety Institute evaluation of Mythos cyber capabilities
Benchmark results: 73% success rate on expert-level CTF tasks
Detailed analysis of vulnerability types discovered in testing
Assessment of dual-use risk and containment feasibility
Business consulting perspective on Mythos as an enterprise wake-up call
Strategic recommendations for enterprises not in Project Glasswing
Analysis of the competitive cybersecurity market shift triggered by Mythos
Coverage of the 68% of organizations impacted by AI-related data leaks

stats.fact: Claude Mythos Preview achieves a 73% success rate on expert-level capture-the-flag (CTF) cybersecurity tasks — a category where no model could succeed before April 2025
stats.source: AISI (UK AI Safety Institute) evaluation, April 2026

stats.fact: Mythos Preview identified thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across every major operating system and web browser in pre-release testing; 99% were undefended at the April 7 announcement date
Mythos discovered a 27-year-old signed integer overflow vulnerability in OpenBSD's SACK TCP implementation that survived decades of human code review and millions of automated security tests
The model completed a 32-step corporate network attack simulation — a task estimated at 20 hours for human experts — in 3 of 10 controlled attempts
Anthropic is committing up to $100 million in usage credits for Project Glasswing partners plus $4 million in direct donations to open-source security organizations

Project Glasswing launched with 12 named partners (AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks) plus 40+ additional critical infrastructure organizations
Claude Mythos Preview API pricing for Glasswing partners: $25 per million input tokens / $125 per million output tokens — approximately 5x the cost of Claude Opus 4.7
Mythos detects 17% more vulnerabilities than prior AI systems in controlled security benchmarks
68% of organizations have already experienced data leaks linked to AI usage, yet only 23% have formal governance policies in place
stats.source: Bain & Company, Claude Mythos Cybersecurity Report, 2026

stats.fact: GPT-5.5, announced after Mythos, achieved 71.4% on expert-level narrow cyber tasks vs 68.6% for Mythos Preview — indicating rapid competitive escalation
Engineers at Anthropic with no formal security training successfully used Mythos to find remote code execution vulnerabilities overnight via Claude Code, waking up to complete working exploits
83% accuracy in finding new vulnerabilities across Mythos testing phases, exceeding all prior AI security benchmarks

angles: The dual-use paradox: how Anthropic built the most dangerous offensive AI model and then locked it away for defenders only
Technical deep-dive: how Claude Mythos actually finds zero-day vulnerabilities using agentic loops (hypothesize → test → exploit)
Practical guide: how to position your organization for Project Glasswing access before similar capabilities become widely available
The 27-year bug phenomenon: why AI finds vulnerabilities that survived decades of human review and automated scanning
Claude Mythos vs. GPT-5.5-Cyber vs. Google Big Sleep: the new AI cybersecurity arms race
What Project Glasswing means for open-source maintainers who can't afford enterprise security tools
Enterprise CISO playbook: preparing your security posture for a world where Mythos-level AI is broadly available
